contract.name: v2/autocomplete/awarding_agency.md
contract.description: Autocomplete lookup returning awarding agency candidates with toptier and subtier metadata for a search string.

search_text.description: Text fragment matched against awarding agency names or abbreviations (case-insensitive).
search_text.constraints: Required; rejects null, empty string, or omitted values; whitespace-only strings are accepted and return broad results; numeric and boolean JSON values are coerced to their string representation before matching.

limit.description: Maximum number of matches to include in the response.
limit.constraints: Optional; default 10 when omitted; accepts positive integers, JSON numeric values (floats are truncated toward zero), and integer-like strings; decimal strings such as '2.9' are rejected with HTTP 400; zero yields an empty result set; negative numbers trigger a 500 HTML error; no server-imposed maximum observed up to 500.

results.description: Autocomplete matches including agency identifiers and toptier/subtier metadata.

id.description: Internal USAspending identifier for the matched agency.

toptier_flag.description: True when the match represents a toptier agency entry.

toptier_code.description: Three-digit code assigned to the toptier agency.

abbreviation.description: Toptier agency abbreviation; observed as uppercase strings or null.
Subtier abbreviation; can be null when no abbreviation is recorded.

messages.description: Informational strings accompanying the response; currently always contains the deprecation warning.

messages: This endpoint is DEPRECATED. Please refer to the api contracts.

body.detail: Missing one or more required request parameters: search_text
Limit request parameter is not a valid, positive integer

quirks: Responses always include a deprecation warning string in the messages array, even when no matches are found.
Limit accepts JSON floats and integer-like strings, truncating floats toward zero, but decimal strings such as '2.9' return HTTP 400.
Negative limit values crash the server with an HTML 500 response.
Whitespace-only search_text values are treated as valid and return a broad list of agencies.
Non-string search_text scalars are coerced to strings, so malformed payloads may succeed but return no matches.
risks: Endpoint is deprecated and may be removed or changed without notice.
Clients must guard against negative limit values to avoid 500 HTML errors.
Large limits can return hundreds of agencies; callers should cap responses to control payload size.
Decimal string values for limit are rejected even though JSON floats truncate, so clients should sanitize string inputs.
Because non-string search_text values are coerced, unhealthy payloads may silently return zero results instead of failing.

response.bodyExcerpt: {"results":[{"id":1136},{"id":1527},...],"messages":["This endpoint is DEPRECATED. Please refer to the api contracts."]}

probes.notes: whitespace-only search returns broad list of agencies.

mismatches: Docs describe limit as a generic number, but the API enforces positive integers: decimal strings are rejected with 400 and negatives trigger a 500 HTML error.
risks: Endpoint is deprecated and may be removed or changed without notice.
Negative limit inputs can crash the server; callers must validate before sending.
Large limits can return hundreds of rows, so clients should cap the size to control payload.
